multiplechoiceresponse.label: In fairness-aware logistic regression, labels may be flipped using \[Y_i^{\prime}=Y_i\left(1-2 Z_i\right).\] What happens when \(Z_i=1\)?

choicegroup.choice: The label is set to zero, removing its effect on training.
The observation is downweighted rather than changed.
The label is flipped (positive → negative or negative → positive).

div.p: Explanation: When \(Z_i=1\), the multiplier becomes \(1-2(1)=-1\). As a result, \(Y_i^{\prime}=-Y_i\), which flips the label from \(+1\) to \(-1\) or from \(-1\) to \(+1\).This matches the lecture's description of selectively flipping outcome labels to achieve demographic parity. The operation changes the label itself, not the weight or inclusion of the observation.
